content.problemStatement:
Chef Ada loves trees, she thinks that the beauty of a tree is determined by the distance between its center and centroid. If the tree has multiple centers or centroids, then the beauty is the maximum among every possible pair.



As a reward for her delicious dishes (and awesome coding skills), CodeChef is going to give a present to Chef Ada, and of course it must be a tree! But not any tree, we want to give her one with **n** nodes, and beauty **b**. Help find such a tree.
content.inputFormat: The first line of input contains one number T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers n and b, the desired number of vertices and beauty.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if no tree satisfies the conditions print 'NO' in a new line. Otherwise, print 'YES' in a new line and describe any valid tree. Print n-1 more lines, each with two integers ui, vi (1 ≤ ui, vi ≤ n) denoting the indices of the vertices connected by an edge in the tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Vertex 1 is the center and centroid of the tree.
Explanation: Vertex 1 is the center and centroid of the tree.
Explanation: Vertex 1 is the center and centroid of the tree.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a simple tree construction approach. We start by checking if the given beauty b is less than or equal to n-1. If it is, we can construct a tree with n vertices and beauty b by creating a star graph with n-1 vertices connected to a central vertex. The central vertex will be both the centroid and the center of the tree. If b is greater than n-1, we cannot construct a tree with the given beauty, so we print 'NO'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*n) — where T is the number of test cases and n is the number of vertices in the tree. The time complexity is linear with respect to the input size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — where n is the number of vertices in the tree. The space complexity is linear with respect to the input size.
